title: validation_completed event (v10 read-side compatibility)
$comment:
LEGACY READ-ONLY. This is the v10 envelope shape for what v11 calls 'spec_validated'. The schema exists so v11 kernels can load existing event logs containing v10 entries without failing. NEW WRITES MUST USE spec_validated. There is no internal code path that emits this event type — only the read-side validator accepts it.

Differences from the canonical v11 envelope (events.v1.json):
  - 'actor' is a STRING (e.g. 'cli'), not a structured object.
  - 'session_id' lives at the TOP LEVEL, not nested in actor.session_id.
  - 'event' is the literal string 'validation_completed' (renamed to 'spec_validated' in v11).

All other envelope fields (seq, ts, spec_id, data, prev_hash, event_hash) match v11. The chain hash was computed by the v10 writer over the v10 canonical JSON; the v11 hash chain semantics are preserved by NOT re-normalizing the parsed event before hash verification.

Authority: this schema is added by KERNEL-EVENT-V10-COMPAT-ALIAS-001 as a narrow read-side compatibility surface. The compat path is invoked ONLY when the input's 'event' field equals 'validation_completed'; all other event names fall through to the canonical v11 validator and behave unchanged.

session_id.description: v10 placed session_id at the top level of the envelope. v11 nests it under actor.session_id. The compat schema admits this top-level placement; the runtime accept logic preserves it verbatim and does not lift it into actor.

actor.description: v10 actor is a string (e.g. 'cli', 'agent', 'user'). v11 actor is a structured { kind, id } object. The compat path preserves the string form verbatim; it does NOT synthesize a v11 actor object during the read.

event.description: Literal v10 event name. The v11 equivalent is 'spec_validated'. New writes MUST use 'spec_validated'; this constant fences the compat path so it cannot accidentally match any other event.

spec_id.description: Spec identifier. validation_completed is spec-scoped (mirrors the REQUIRES_SPEC_ID class of its v11 successor).

data.description: v10 payload. Matches the Sterling-observed shape (seq 117-119 in the v10->v11 migrant repo that motivated this compat alias). The 'passed' field is required; the others are optional because some v10 emitters omitted them.
